heading: Nadgee vs Nambucca Heads — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Nadgee, New South Wales and Nambucca Heads, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Nadgee has a lower overall climate risk score (28/100) compared to Nambucca Heads (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Nadgee or Nambucca Heads safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Nadgee has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Nambucca Heads scores 21/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Nadgee scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. Nambucca Heads scores 37/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Nadgee or Nambucca Heads?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Nadgee has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.33% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
